title: Leakage-Safe OOF Regime Stacker, With Minimal Post-Hoc Calibration
summary: Stacked generalization is a natural next step once the base learners are meaningfully different.
rationale: Stacked generalization is a natural next step once the base learners are meaningfully different. At the same time, calibration literature warns that isotonic/binning methods often improve probability quality at the cost of grouping/discrimination, and scikit-learn’s target-encoding example shows how badly high-cardinality statistics can overfit without cross-fitting.
implementation_hint: Build a level-1 model only from out-of-fold predictions of the baseline classifier, survival model, listwise ranker, and teacher model, plus small regime indicators like wet/dry, race phase, tyre bin, and stint bin. Keep every categorical prior/encoding fold-local, drop any train+test joint frequency statistics, and only test calibration inside the OOF meta-CV if it improves AUC rather than just log loss.
expected_effect: Lets the pipeline learn which base learner is strongest in each regime while avoiding the most likely validation trap: leakage through encoders, priors, or meta-features.
risk: Gain may be small if the base models remain too correlated; meta-features are easy to leak, and heavy calibration can flatten ranking quality.
